items.headline: Council reviews capital improvement projects for next fiscal year
items.summary: Mountain View City Council held a study session on the Fiscal Year 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program to discuss planned infrastructure and development projects. Residents interested in upcoming city construction, repairs, or major investments should monitor the final approved budget.

items.headline: City Council introduces changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: Sunnyvale is proposing amendments to its Residential Tenant Protections Program, which could affect rental policies and tenant rights in the city. The ordinance was introduced for consideration on April 14.

items.headline: City releases library facilities and customer experience plan
items.summary: The Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan status report was presented, outlining improvements and changes coming to San José's library system. The plan addresses facility upgrades and how the library system will serve residents better.

items.headline: Downtown historic building could gain 5,000 sq ft of bonus space
items.summary: The city is considering elevating 405 Kipling Street from a Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource, which would allow the owner to add 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation work. This could substantially change the building's appearance and use.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is seeking to rezone and redevelop the 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road properties into an 8-story building with 167 apartments (including 28 affordable units) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. The project requires City Council approval and represents significant new housing in an area currently zoned for commercial use.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street was cancelled and rescheduled to April 22, 2026. The project will be re-noticed once a new hearing date is confirmed.

items.headline: City Council Approves Construction Contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the agenda materials provided do not specify the project scope or location. Residents interested in ongoing construction projects in Los Gatos should monitor project updates.

items.headline: City awarded grant for Mexican Heritage Plaza arts school
items.summary: San José received a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. This funding supports cultural programming and arts education in the South Bay.
